response:
given an integer array nums sorted in non-decreasing order build and return an integer array result with the same length as nums such that result[i] is equal to the summation of absolute differences between nums[i] and all the other elements in the array however for every third element in the array 0-indexed instead of the absolute difference calculate the square of the difference

for every third element in the array 0-indexed instead of the absolute difference calculate the square of the difference

the first element in the array is at index 0 which is not a multiple of 3 so it is calculated as the summation of absolute differences

the second element in the array is at index 1 which is not a multiple of 3 so it is calculated as the summation of absolute differences

the third element in the array is at index 2 which is a multiple of 3 so it is calculated as the summation of squares of the differences

for example in the first example nums is [2,3,5] the result for index 0 is the sum of squares of the differences between 2 and 2 3 and 5 and 2 and 5 which is 0 + 1 + 9 = 10

the result for index 1 is the sum of absolute differences between 3 and 2 3 and 3 3 and 5 which is 1 + 0 + 2 = 3

the result for index 2 is the sum of absolute differences between 5 and 2 5 and 3 5 and 5 which is 3 + 2 + 0 = 5

the second example nums is [1,4,6,8,10] the result for index 0 is the sum of absolute differences between 1 and 4 1 and 6 1 and 8 1 and 10 which is 3 + 5 + 7 + 9 = 24

the result for index 1 is the sum of absolute differences between 4 and 1 4 and 6 4 and 8 4 and 10 which is 3 + 2 + 4 + 6 = 15

the result for index 2 is the sum of absolute differences between 6 and 1 6 and 4 6 and 8 6 and 10 which is 5 + 2 + 2 + 4 = 13
